system_prompt: You are an expert Go/Golang developer. When reviewing code, you identify bugs clearly, explain why they are problematic, and provide corrected versions with best practices.

categories.name: nil_pointer
bug_patterns: Accessing fields on a nil pointer returned from a function without error checking
Using type assertion without comma-ok pattern leading to nil panic
Dereferencing a pointer in a struct field without nil check
Calling methods on an interface value that might be nil
Accessing map values that return zero-value pointers

categories.name: goroutine_leak
bug_patterns: Goroutine blocked on channel send with no receiver
Goroutine in infinite loop without context cancellation check
Timer/ticker not stopped causing goroutine to never exit
HTTP request in goroutine without timeout or context
Goroutine waiting on WaitGroup that is never Done()

categories.name: race_condition
categories.description: Data race and race condition bugs
bug_patterns: Multiple goroutines writing to a shared map without synchronization
Reading and writing a shared variable without mutex protection
Closing a channel from multiple goroutines
Appending to a shared slice from concurrent goroutines
Using a shared counter without atomic operations

categories.name: resource_leak
bug_patterns: HTTP response body not closed after reading
File handle not closed in error paths
Database connection not returned to pool
TCP connection not closed on error
Context cancel function not called (defer missing)

categories.name: error_handling
bug_patterns: Ignoring errors with blank identifier (_, _ = ...)
Wrapping errors without %w verb losing the error chain
Checking error string instead of using errors.Is/errors.As
Returning early without cleaning up resources on error
Shadowing err variable in nested scope causing wrong error to propagate

categories.name: slice_map_pitfalls
bug_patterns: Modifying a slice while iterating over it with range
Assuming slice append always modifies the original (capacity exceeded)
Using a map with struct pointer keys causing unexpected behavior
Not initializing a map before writing to it (nil map panic)
Taking address of range variable causing all pointers to point to last element

categories.name: concurrency_mistakes
bug_patterns: Using defer inside a for loop causing resource accumulation
WaitGroup Add() called inside goroutine instead of before
Missing default case in select causing unexpected blocking
Copying sync.Mutex by value instead of using pointer
Using time.Sleep for synchronization instead of proper primitives

categories.name: api_misuse
bug_patterns: Using json.Unmarshal with unexported struct fields
Creating HTTP client without timeout (uses no timeout by default)
Using fmt.Sprintf for building SQL queries (SQL injection)
Calling os.Exit in a function that should return an error
Using strings.Replace with n=-1 when strings.ReplaceAll exists

generation_instruction: For each bug pattern: 1) Generate a realistic buggy code snippet (10-30 lines), 2) Ask the user to review and identify the bug, 3) In the response, clearly identify the bug with explanation of WHY it's dangerous, 4) Provide the corrected code with comments explaining the fix. Each example should be self-contained and realistic.
